title: String Manipulation
description: Program to perform various operations on a user's full name.
explanation: The program extracts first and last name, reverses them, converts case, and prints formatted messages.
code:
name = input('Enter full name: ')
parts = name.split()
first = parts[0]
last = parts[-1]
print('First Name:', first)
print('Last Name:', last)
print('Reverse Order:', last + ', ' + first)
print('Uppercase:', name.upper())
print('Lowercase:', name.lower())
print(f'Welcome {first}!')
print('Welcome {}'.format(first))

title: Numeric Operations
description: Program to perform arithmetic operations on integer, float and numeric string.
explanation: The program converts string to numeric types and performs arithmetic calculations demonstrating operator precedence.
code:
a = int(input('Enter integer: '))
b = float(input('Enter float: '))
c = input('Enter numeric string: ')
c_int = int(c)
c_float = float(c)
print('Sum:', a + b + c_float)
print('Difference:', a - b - c_float)
print('Product:', a * b * c_float)
print('Division:', a / b)
print('Operator precedence:', a + b * c_int ** 2)

title: Leap Year Check
description: Program to determine whether a year is leap year or not.
explanation: A year is leap if divisible by 4 and not 100 unless divisible by 400.

title: Fibonacci Sequence
description: Program to generate Fibonacci series up to n terms.
explanation: Uses a loop to generate sequence where each term is sum of previous two.
code:
n = int(input('Enter number of terms: '))
a = 0
b = 1
for i in range(n):
    print(a, end=' ')
    a, b = b, a + b

title: Student Database Using List and Tuple
description: Program to manage student records using list of tuples.
explanation: Provides menu to add, search, remove and compute average marks.

title: Dictionary and Set Operations
description: Program to manage employee records using dictionary and perform set operations.
explanation: Demonstrates dictionary CRUD operations and set union, intersection, difference.

title: NumPy Array Operations
description: Program to demonstrate NumPy array arithmetic and slicing.
explanation: Performs element-wise operations and statistical calculations using NumPy.
code:
import numpy as np
a = np.array(list(map(int, input('Enter array 1: ').split())))
b = np.array(list(map(int, input('Enter array 2: ').split())))
print('Addition:', a + b)
print('Subtraction:', a - b)
print('Multiplication:', a * b)
print('Division:', a / b)
print('Sum:', a.sum())
print('Mean:', a.mean())
print('Max:', a.max())
print('Min:', a.min())
print('Slice:', a[:3])

title: Pandas CSV Processing
description: Program to read and analyze CSV file using pandas.
explanation: Reads student marks from CSV and filters high scorers.
code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv('students.csv')
print(df.head())
print(df.tail())
high = df[df['Marks'] > 80]
print(high)
print(df.mean())
df.to_csv('output.csv', index=False)

title: Matplotlib Visualization
description: Program to create bar, pie and line charts.
explanation: Uses matplotlib to visualize sales data.
code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
names = input('Products: ').split()
values = list(map(int, input('Sales: ').split()))
plt.bar(names, values)
plt.title('Bar Chart')
plt.show()
plt.pie(values, labels=names, autopct='%1.1f%%')
plt.show()
plt.plot(values)
plt.show()

title: Exploratory Data Analysis
description: Program to perform basic EDA on dataset.
explanation: Loads dataset, cleans missing values and visualizes distributions.
code:
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
df = pd.read_csv('data.csv')
print(df.describe())
df = df.dropna()
df.hist()
plt.show()
